title: 帷幕
summary:
Hercule Poirot and Hastings discuss the five murder cases detailed in the case summary. Poirot observes that while the cases involve different motives—such as jealousy, money, and selflessness—each has only one clear suspect. He reveals a common thread: a mysterious individual referred to as "X" was present near the victims or their locations in all five instances, despite having no motive and being hundreds of miles away when the actual crimes occurred. Hastings concludes that "X" is the true killer.

Poirot informs Hastings that "X" is currently present at Styles Manor and predicts that a murder will soon occur in the house. When Hastings suggests that Poirot can prevent the crime, Poirot explains the difficulty of such an intervention. He outlines three potential methods: warning the victim, which often fails due to disbelief; or using cunning to intervene at the exact moment of the strike to catch the murderer in the act. Poirot notes that they do not yet know who the target will be because the motive remains unknown. Although Hastings wonders if the motive is revenge, Poirot refuses to reveal the identity of "X" to Hastings to prevent him from reacting visibly when they encounter the suspect.

The conversation is interrupted by the arrival of Hastings' daughter, Judith. Described as tall with a serious, tragic air, she is a scientific graduate. Poirot teases her about her work, noting that her fingers are stained with methylene blue.
